summary: Iteration 16 Phase 16 refactor + SDK abstraction verification is GREEN. Confirmed 4703 -> 632 line server.py refactor caused zero /api/* regressions. Wrote /app/backend/tests/test_iter16_phase16.py (37 tests, 37/37 PASS) covering (a) /api/health integrations dict with all 4 fallback values (llm=emergent_proxy, email=sent_stub, sms=sent_stub, google_oauth_direct=false), (b) direct Google OAuth authorize+callback returning graceful 503 when env vars missing, (c) Emergent /auth/google/session still functional (400 on missing header, not 500), (d) LLM abstraction via /api/forms/transcribe + /api/protocols/transcribe returning 200 (proves llm_client.complete_text() routes through emergent proxy), (e) notifier abstraction via /api/forms/send channel=email|sms returning delivery_status='sent_stub' AND writing _stubbed=True to integration_log (verified in Mongo), (f) representative endpoint per every extracted router (clients/admin/appointments/memberships/invoices/plans/reminders/symptoms/labs/messages/treatments/inventory/POS/transactions/timeclock/frontdesk/telehealth/forms/soap-templates/protocols/compliance) each responding 200, (g) NIST password validator emits clean 400 on both length-short and name-contains rules. Full regression: 207 passed + 1 skipped + 1 known pre-existing failure (test_phase4::test_change_password_and_revert — 'from backend.auth_utils' import bug, explicitly deselected per request). Nine test_phase6 WebSocket errors on the full-run were transient handshake timeouts under load; standalone rerun 17/17 GREEN. Frontend smoke: all 5 role logins land on correct portal (admin -> /portal/admin with all 6 metric tiles rendered: Clients 201, Users 112, Visit Notes 25, Files 2, Appt Requests 3, Audit Events 1800; practitioner -> /portal/provider; auditor -> /portal/admin/audit with Audit Log fully rendering & no infinite loop). Login page shows 'Sign in with Google' Emergent-managed button (since /api/health.integrations.google_oauth_direct=false). /oauth-complete route renders 'Sign-in failed / Missing tokens' card without JS crash when navigated to directly.

minor.issue: 9 tests intermittently fail with 'timed out during opening handshake' under full-suite parallel load; standalone re-run passes 17/17. Not a router-level regression, just a load-sensitive ws timeout. Consider bumping the client-side ws timeout in test_phase6.py or adding pytest-rerunfailures.

action_items: None required. All Phase 16 exit criteria met: (a) server.py 632 lines, all routes extracted; (b) /api/health integrations dict correctly reports all 4 vendor statuses; (c) LLM + notifier abstractions correctly fall back to Emergent proxy / sent_stub when direct-vendor env vars absent; (d) direct Google OAuth endpoints exist and return graceful 503 (not 500 crash) when env vars missing; (e) Emergent Google SSO not broken by adding direct path; (f) frontend admin/practitioner/auditor logins all land on correct portals with no console spam.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py still contains PUBLIC endpoints (appointment-request, vip-signup) that log to integration_log with hard-coded '[SENDGRID STUB]' log line + manual insert of _stubbed:True — these should be switched to notifiers.send_email() so admins see them in the same audit trail as forms/reminders. Minor cleanup, not blocking.
notifiers.py send_email early-returns on `email_status() != 'live'` BEFORE reading SENDGRID_FROM_EMAIL/SENDGRID_API_KEY from env — good defensive layering. Same for send_sms. No env-key leakage risk.

auth.py google_oauth_callback ends with RedirectResponse to `${FRONTEND_ORIGIN}/oauth-complete?access=<jwt>&refresh=<jwt>` — putting the JWT in the URL query is a P0 security concern because it lands in browser history + any downstream referer logs. Recommend switching to setting HttpOnly cookies OR posting the tokens to a short-lived server-side session key that the frontend then exchanges via a POST. This is not yet activated (env vars empty) but should be fixed before the first Google BAA-live customer.

llm_client.py has no timeout on the direct-Anthropic AsyncAnthropic call — under a stuck upstream it can hold the request loop indefinitely. Add `timeout=` to AsyncAnthropic constructor or wrap in `asyncio.wait_for(..., timeout=90)`.

test_phase4.py still has `from backend.auth_utils` import inside test_change_password_and_revert — 6+ iterations old. Either rewrite to use pymongo directly (like our new iter16 revert script) or delete the whole test. Every future full-suite run will show 1 failure until this is cleaned up.

server.py imports every router as `_auth_routes` etc. side-effect style. Consider a single explicit include list at top of file so a `git blame` shows which router owns which prefix without having to grep across 9 files.

success_rate.backend: 100% for iteration 16 phase 16 suite (37/37); 99.5% for full-suite regression (207/208 excluding known pre-existing test_phase4::test_change_password_and_revert import bug; 9 additional test_phase6 ws errors are transient — clean 17/17 on standalone rerun)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (all 5 role logins land correctly, /oauth-complete renders without crash, admin portal shows all metric tiles)

test_credentials: Admin: admin@natmedsol.local / Admin!2345 (RESTORED — see RCA below). Alt admin: tallyravello@gmail.com / TEST123. Practitioner: ravello@natmedsol.local / Ravello!2345. Staff: frontdesk@natmedsol.local / FrontDesk!2345. Auditor: auditor@natmedsol.local / Auditor!2345.
seed_data_creation: During test_iter16_phase16.py::TestNotifierAbstraction 2 stubbed integration_log entries were written (service=sendgrid action=form.email and service=twilio action=form.sms, both _stubbed=True) — verified in Mongo. 2 form_submission docs were created via /api/forms/send with delivery_status='sent_stub'. All cleanly tagged; no impact on production seed data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 16 refactor + SDK abstraction is verified GREEN. To re-run: `REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=<url> pytest /app/backend/tests/test_iter16_phase16.py -v` (37 tests, ~16s). For full backend regression skip test_phase7 + test_phase10_forms + test_phase4::TestAuthAndAccount::test_change_password_and_revert. test_phase6 (telehealth ws) is flaky under full-suite parallel load — either run standalone OR add pytest-rerunfailures. The 4 mock/stub paths are: (1) LLM => emergent_proxy fallback (works, will flip to anthropic_direct when ANTHROPIC_API_KEY set); (2) SendGrid => sent_stub fallback (works); (3) Twilio => sent_stub fallback (works); (4) Direct Google OAuth => 503 graceful (works, will flip to real flow when GOOGLE_OAUTH_* env vars set). To activate any of the 4 live paths, set the corresponding env var(s) in /app/backend/.env and restart. /api/health should then flip that key from stub/proxy/false to live/anthropic_direct/true.
rca of the issue: During test authoring, one attempt to verify the common-password rule accidentally set admin@natmedsol.local's password to 'Password1234' (validator did not reject 12-char 'Password1234' — it passed length + name-contains checks). Reverted immediately by running a one-shot python that computed hash_password('Admin!2345') via auth_utils and pymongo-updated users.password_hash directly. Verified restoration with a fresh POST /api/auth/login (200). Test now uses 'administrator2026!' which is guaranteed rejected by the name-contains rule (admin fragment in email) — safe against future accidental mutations. NOTE for main agent: consider adding a common-password blocklist (like the top-1000 breached list) to auth_utils.validate_password_strength — 'Password1234' getting through is a NIST rule miss even though it passes length + name-not-in checks.
